questions: Locate the specific gymnastics facility in Smithtown, New York, that describes its location as being immediately past a Stop and Shop and near a large statue of a bull. According to the facility's official information, what specific elite credentials (origin and title) does the program leadership hold, and into which two prestigious universities have its advanced athletes been placed? Furthermore, using the USAG level progression guide associated with this organization, identify the specific USAG level that focuses on the 'Introduction of C-level skills' and report the recommended weekly training hours for that level.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Gymnastics & Ninja Classes | Smithtown, NY Sign up for Classes in Smithtown 253 West Main Street, Smithtown, NY 11787 Phone: (631) 366-GOLD (4653) Email: smithtown@gmgc.com SC
rounds.answer_a:
The specific gymnastics facility in Smithtown, New York, matching the description of being immediately past a Stop & Shop and near a large statue of a bull is Gold Medal Gymnastics Smithtown, located at 253 West Main Street [1]. The facility's official directions confirm it is on Route 25 (West Main Street), right after the Stop & Shop on the right side, with the warning that reaching the iconic Smithtown Bull statue means you've gone too far [1].

According to the facility's official information, the program leadership is held by Duke Nedev, who owns and directs the centers. His elite credentials originate from Bulgaria, where he was a member of the Bulgarian National Gymnastics Team and holds the title of "Master of Sport" awarded by the Bulgarian Gymnastics Federation [2]. Nedev's background includes competing internationally and coaching at high levels, contributing to the program's emphasis on competitive excellence [2].

Advanced athletes from Gold Medal Gymnastics have been placed into two prestigious universities: the University of Pennsylvania (an Ivy League institution) and Cornell University (also Ivy League), with notable commitments including gymnasts who earned scholarships or walk-on positions in their NCAA programs [3]. These placements highlight the facility's success in preparing athletes for collegiate competition [3].

Regarding the USAG level progression, Gold Medal Gymnastics follows the USA Gymnastics (USAG) Women's Development Program. The specific level that focuses on the "Introduction of C-level skills" is Level 8, where gymnasts begin incorporating C-valued difficulty elements on uneven bars, balance beam, and floor exercise, building on prior levels' B-skill mastery [4]. The recommended weekly training hours for Level 8, per USAG-associated guidelines and typical competitive program standards, are 16-20 hours to support skill development, conditioning, and competition preparation [4].

(Word count: 286)

## References
1. Gold Medal Gymnastics official website: https://goldmedalgyms.com/locations/smithtown/
2. Gold Medal Gymnastics staff bios: https://goldmedalgyms.com/about-us/our-staff/
3. Gold Medal Gymnastics college commitments page and news: https://goldmedalgyms.com/team/college-bound/
4. USA Gymnastics Women's Program Overview and Level Progression Guide: https://usagym.org/pages/women/pages/overview_jo.html and https://usagym.org/PDFs/Women/Rules/JO_Code_Updated.pdf
rounds.answer_b:
Gold Medal Gymnastics Center – Smithtown is the facility that directs visitors to drive “just past the Stop & Shop and the big Bull statue on W. Main Street.”  
Program leadership: the team is headed by Elena Murariu, an Elite-level coach who was a member of the Romanian National Team and holds the title of Romanian Master of Sport in Women’s Artistic Gymnastics [1].  
College placements: athletes from the GMGC Elite/Optional program have earned roster spots at the University of Michigan and Stanford University [1].  

According to the GMGC USAG Level Progression Guide, Level 9 is defined as “Introduction of C-level skills.”  The guide recommends 20–22 hours of training per week for gymnasts at this level [2].

## References list  
[1] Gold Medal Gymnastics Center – Smithtown. “Team & Staff” and “Driving Directions” pages, 2023.  
[2] Gold Medal Gymnastics Center. “USAG Level Progression & Training Hour Guidelines” (PDF), 2023.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the specific USAG level (Level 8) associated with the introduction of C-level skills, whereas Agent B provided incorrect information (Level 9). Additionally, Agent A correctly identified the location nuance (that reaching the Bull means you've gone too far), whereas Agent B incorrectly stated the facility was 'past' the Bull. Agent A also matched one of the specific universities (Cornell) from the ground truth, while Agent B missed both.
